frameworks.name: multivariate pattern decoding
frameworks.one_line: fMRI MVPA used to read out content of parietal visual representations

frameworks.name: two-stream (dorsal/ventral) visual processing
frameworks.one_line: extends dorsal-stream role beyond spatial attention to abstract object codes

frameworks.name: action-relevant visual coding
frameworks.one_line: parietal code framed as complementary action-oriented visual representation

core_question: What range of visual stimulus properties is represented in the human parietal cortex, and how do those representations differ from ventral-stream codes?
key_assumptions: fMRI multivariate decoding indexes content of cortical representations
parietal cortex traditionally seen as handling spatial/attentional properties
ventral stream is the canonical substrate for object representation
key_findings: parietal cortex decodes object category beyond spatial properties
parietal cortex decodes location, size, and viewpoint of objects
parietal representations are more abstract than ventral-stream representations
interpretations: parietal cortex contributes a complementary, action-relevant visual code
its role exceeds spatial attention alone
